objective: Make Today feel more like the full local daily loop by letting the user save a browser-local day review checkpoint and archive the day from Today, without navigating to Patterns.
riskClass: Q3 because this goal lane includes approved production deployment after local proof; app code itself is Q2 browser-local frontend state.
approvalStatus: Production promotion was previously approved by user message: PRODUCTION_DEPLOY_APPROVED=true promote. This slice does not edit credentials, auth, DNS, backend services, n8n, Telegram, Postgres, public MCP, or WHOOP settings.
truthBoundary: Today day review and archive are browser-local demo behavior only.
No WHOOP, accounts, hosted AI, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Postgres, backend writes, reminders, timers, or cross-device sync are connected or claimed.
The separate LifeMax lab/source boot reports remain red, so connected daily usability is not claimed.
defectsFound: A user could run most of the daily loop on Today, but the save review/archive day ending lived under Patterns, making Today feel less like the actual operating surface.
The visible end-of-day proof path required navigation to Patterns even though the action describes today's completed loop.
Initial in-app browser smoke attempted to read textarea values as rendered text/attributes; the smoke was repaired to verify visible capture state and downstream archive behavior instead.
repairsMade: Added Today-local review checkpoint and archive handlers that reuse deriveTodayView and createDayArchive.
Rendered the existing DayReviewPanel on Today after the evening close, so the daily loop can be saved and archived from the main screen.
Added Today-specific status messages that explicitly say the review/history write stays in this browser and is not sent to a server.
Added a visual smoke test covering check-in, generated plan, capture, evening close, Today review save, Today archive, refresh persistence, and Patterns history visibility.

localChecks.detail: Next.js 16.2.7 static build generated 10 routes including /today, /capture, /patterns, /experiments, /profile, /privacy

localChecks.command: rg secret-pattern scan over changed source/test files

browserLocal.server: npm run start -- --hostname 127.0.0.1 --port 3000 after production build
flow: Open /today, save check-in, and generate a browser-local plan.
Open /capture, save a helped habit capture, and verify the capture record exists.
Return to /today, save the evening close, and verify Local day review ready.
Click Save review checkpoint from Today and verify the browser-local status message.
Click Save to history from Today and verify the browser-local archive status message.
Refresh /today and verify 1 archived local day persists.
Open /patterns and verify Local day history shows 1 local day archived.
Open /today at 1280x800 and verify no horizontal overflow.

labBoundary.bootPacket: red at 2026-06-20T12:59:51.707Z: safe_to_claim_daily_usable false; local preflight red; shared-context reports stale

residualRisks: This is still browser-local demo behavior; real backend/account/integration readiness is unverified and currently red in lab boot artifacts.
Today is more end-to-end, but broader progress-board hierarchy and memory provenance are still candidate future improvements.
Production smoke is still pending in this packet and must be added before claiming the deployed slice is proven.
rollbackPath: Git rollback: revert the Today day-review commit and redeploy the previous production commit if needed.
Runtime rollback: promote the previous Vercel production deployment. No database/auth/credential migration is part of this slice.
User-data rollback is not needed for backend records because this feature writes only browser-local demo state.

contextScout.topGap: Make /today feel like a real progress board/full daily loop rather than a stack of cards.
contextScout.decision: Implemented the end-of-day review/archive action on Today as the smallest high-impact daily-loop closure.
